LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- DEFENDANT THE ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION UNTIL ARBITRATION IS COMPLETED IS GRANTED. EVEN THE AGREEMENT WERE PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY. ARBITRATION IS COMPELLED AND THIS CASE IS STAYED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, COURT REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Jun-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Paul E. Gaspari, Esq., Tobin & Tobin, 415-772-9618, for defendant and moving party
Jeremy Pasternak, Esq., Law Offices, 415-693-0300, for plaintiff and opposing party

ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT THE ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action until arbitration is completed is Granted. Even the agreement were procedurally unconscionable, plaintiff has not shown substantive unconscionability. Arbitration is compelled and this case is stayed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Court Reporter: Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

PROOF OF SERVICE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2011 Off Calendar
